content:
type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: image_to_base64() model_validator + new get_image_mimetype() method
code:     def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the image bytes
        using the filetype library and cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype and self.image:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we wont load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self
note: Placing get_image_mimetype() right before image_to_base64(); image_to_base64 now calls normalize_image_bytes(self.image) (your helper) then self.get_image_mimetype() (mine) instead of inline b64encode+filetype.guess. Need your exact normalize_image_bytes() signature/body to finalize order/behavior — please send yours as a DRAFT too.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: get_image_mimetype() method + image_to_base64() validator (keeping your normalize_image_bytes() as-is)
code:     def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the image bytes
        using the filetype library and cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype and self.image:
            decoded = base64.b64decode(self.image)
            guess = filetype.guess(decoded)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we wont load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self
note: get_image_mimetype() must stay no-arg + self-contained (check cache -> guess from self.image -> cache) per my task spec, so any external component can call block.get_image_mimetype() directly without conversion. It decodes self.image itself (post-normalize it is always base64). Your normalize_image_bytes() stays module-level unchanged; image_to_base64 calls it then calls self.get_image_mimetype(). We drop the decoded_for_guessing return usage here (minor redundant decode is fine) unless you feel strongly - let me know if OK.

content:
type: AGREE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: normalize_image_bytes() helper + get_image_mimetype() method + image_to_base64() validator
code: import base64
import binascii
...

def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where encoded_image
    is guaranteed to be base64-encoded and decoded_for_guessing is the raw bytes
    suitable for MIME type detection.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    ...

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the given decoded
        bytes (or self.image if not provided) using the filetype library and
        cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        guess_source = decoded_image if decoded_image is not None else self.image
        if not self.image_mimetype and guess_source:
            guess = filetype.guess(guess_source)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we wont load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
note: Agreed. Will implement verbatim: add import binascii, module-level normalize_image_bytes(), get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image=None), image_to_base64() calling both.

import base64
import binascii
...

def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where encoded_image
    is guaranteed to be base64-encoded and decoded_for_guessing is the raw bytes
    suitable for MIME type detection.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    ...

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the given decoded
        bytes (or self.image if not provided) using the filetype library and
        cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        guess_source = decoded_image if decoded_image is not None else self.image
        if not self.image_mimetype and guess_source:
            guess = filetype.guess(guess_source)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we wont load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self

files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: CONFLICT RESOLUTION: get_image_mimetype() + image_to_base64()
code: Two of our messages crossed: you sent a DRAFT with get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image=None) that decodes explicitly passed bytes (falling back to self.image), and I sent AGREE to that version. But you ALSO sent an AGREE (timestamp 1783546637) accepting the simpler no-arg get_image_mimetype(self) that does base64.b64decode(self.image) internally, with image_to_base64 calling self.get_image_mimetype() with no args and normalize_image_bytes(self.image) discarding the second tuple element via underscore.

Please pick ONE. I am fine with either. Proposing we go with your LATER AGREE (the simpler no-arg version, since the decoded value from normalize_image_bytes is discarded there) to reduce API surface:

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the mimetype of the image.

        If the mimetype has not been set yet, guess it from the image bytes
        using the filetype library and cache the result for future accesses.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype and self.image:
            decoded = base64.b64decode(self.image)
            guess = filetype.guess(decoded)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we wont load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self

Please confirm with a final AGREE echoing this exact code so we both lock it in.
